title: NotificationTemplate
description:
Versioned reusable template. `tenant_id NULL` = platform default (is_system=true); `tenant_id` set = tenant override. Resolver picks tenant override first, falls back to platform default. Body is pre-rendered HTML with Blade placeholders — the React Email JSX renders at CI build time via packages/notifications-emails-renderer. New version bump on every update; publish transitions the version to `state=published`. Older published versions are retained 7 years cold for SOC 2 CC8.1 change-management evidence.

Translation strategy — row-per-locale (NOT `HasTranslations`). Each locale is its OWN row, with an independent `state` (draft/published/archived) + `version` + `body_rendered_html`. This satisfies the tier-A obligation (both `subject_template` + `body_rendered_html` are translatable) without stuffing full HTML bodies of every locale into a single JSONB blob: templates are large, versioned per-locale, and the CI-rendered artefacts differ per locale (RTL layouts, per-locale image assets, provider-specific templates). See `localization/readme.md` §9 for the tier-A governance note.

tenant_id.description: NULL = platform default. tenant_id set = tenant override for the same (key, channel, locale) triple.

key.description: Module-namespaced template key. Typically matches the category slug (`invitations.invitation_sent`). Multiple versions per key exist — the resolver picks the highest `published` version.

category_slug.description: Denormalised from key when it maps 1:1; kept explicit for filtering + admin UX.

locale.description: ISO 639-1 (`en`, `ar`, `fr`, `de`, `es`). Falls back to `en` when the recipient's locale is not available.

version.description: Monotonic per (tenant_id, key, channel, locale). Bumped on every save; unique constraint enforces.

is_system.description: True for platform-default templates seeded by SeedTemplatesCommand. HasSystemFlag refuses edit for non-super_admin.

slug.description: Sluggable-generated. Composite: `{key}-{channel}-{locale}-v{version}` — human-readable for admin UI + import/export filenames.

subject_template.description: Blade template string for the subject line. Applies only to `mail` + `in_app` channels. Rendered at send time with Blade::render().

body_rendered_html.description: Pre-rendered HTML with Blade placeholders. Produced by the emails renderer at CI build time from the .tsx source. Rendered at send time with Blade::render().

provider_template_id.description: Optional provider-side template id (SendGrid Dynamic Templates, Mailgun stored template). When set, the channel module can dispatch by reference instead of rendering + attaching the body.

variables_schema.description: JSON schema for the variables the template expects. Enforced at dispatch time — DispatchGateway refuses payloads that fail schema.

sort_order.description: From Sortable. Ordering within a (key, channel, locale) group when listing versions.

indexes.name: notification_templates_version_unique
indexes.description: One row per (tenant_id, key, channel, locale, version) tuple. Version bumps on save.

indexes.name: notification_templates_resolver_index
indexes.description: Primary resolver read path. Combined with `WHERE state='published' ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1`.

x-translatable.reason: Templates are large (rendered HTML bodies) + versioned per locale (draft/published/archived state is independent per locale) + can differ structurally per locale (RTL layouts, per-locale image assets). JSONB blob storage would duplicate state machinery per locale in one row and lose the per-locale version history.
x-translatable.resolver: Look up (tenant_id, key, channel, locale) WHERE state='published' ORDER BY version DESC LIMIT 1. Fallback to (tenant_id=NULL, key, channel, locale). Fallback to (tenant_id, key, channel, 'en'). Fallback to (tenant_id=NULL, key, channel, 'en').
